document.title: IC Partial Test Report
standards: FCC KDB 558074 D01 DTS Meas Guidance v04
FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01

sourceSummary.oneLine: IC Partial Test Report CR740701-02AC Rev. 01 for Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module under RSS-247.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory evidence that the tested sample was evaluated for radiated unwanted emissions against RSS-247 and related standards, with the reported result marked Pass.
keyPoints: Report No. CR740701-02AC, Report Version Rev. 01, initial issue dated Nov. 29, 2017.
Equipment is identified as an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module, Model No. ST60-2230C-P.
The report lists Laird Technologies as brand name and applicant.
The report states the tested sample was evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the listed standards.
Summary of Test Results lists radiated emissions under RSS-247 Section 5.5 and RSS-Gen Section 8.9 with result Pass.

evidence.claim: The document is an IC Partial Test Report.
The report number is CR740701-02AC.
The report version is Rev. 01.
The release record identifies Rev. 01 as the initial issue dated Nov. 29, 2017.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Record Report No. Version Description Issued Date CR740701-02AC Rev. 01 Initial issue Nov. 29, 2017

evidence.claim: The IC identifier is 3147A-602230C.
The equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The model number is ST60-2230C-P.
The EUT product details list models ST60-2230C-P and SU60-2230C-P.
evidence.evidenceText: The following models are provided to this EUT. Brand Name Model Name Product Name Description Laird Technologies ST60-2230C-P ... SU60-2230C-P

evidence.claim: The brand name and applicant are Laird Technologies.
evidence.evidenceText: Brand Name : Laird Technologies Applicant : Laird Technologies

evidence.claim: The applicant address is W66N220 Commerce Court, Cedarburg, Wisconsin 53012, USA.
evidence.evidenceText: Address : W66N220 Commerce Court, Cedarburg, Wisconsin 53012, USA

evidence.claim: The primary listed standard is RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017.

The report lists RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017, RSS-Gen Issue 4 November 2014, ANSI C63.10-2013, ANSI C63.4-2014, FCC KDB 558074 D01 DTS Meas Guidance v04, and FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01 as applicable standards and KDB documents.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the specification of EUT, the EUT must comply with following standards and KDB documents. RSS-247 Issue 2 February 2017 RSS-Gen Issue 4 November 2014 ANSI C63.10-2013 ANSI C63.4-2014 FCC KDB 558074 D01 DTS Meas Guidance v04 FCC KDB 662911 D01 Multiple Transmitter Output v02r01

evidence.claim: The received date is Sep. 27, 2017.
The tested date range is Oct. 02 to Oct. 03, 2017.
International Certification Corp. declared the tested sample evaluated and in compliance with the requirements of the listed standards.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: Summary of test results reports Radiated Emissions at 812.83 MHz with emission level 44.78 dBuV/m at 3 m, margin -1.22 dB, QP detector, and result Pass.
The radiated emissions summary references RSS-247 Section 5.5 and RSS-Gen Section 8.9.
evidence.evidenceText: RSS-247 Section 5.5 RSS-Gen Section 8.9 Radiated Emissions [dBuV/m at 3m]: 812.83MHz 44.78 (Margin -1.22dB) - QP Pass

evidence.claim: The EUT frequency range for 802.11 b/g/n HT20 is 2400-2483.5 MHz with channel frequencies 2412-2462 MHz.
The EUT frequency range for 802.11n HT40 is 2400-2483.5 MHz with channel frequencies 2422-2452 MHz.
The antenna listed is Laird MAF95310 Mini NanoBlade Flex PCB Dipole with IPEX U.FL connector.
evidence.evidenceText: Laird MAF95310 Mini NanoBlade Flex PCB Dipole IPEX U.FL

evidence.claim: The EUT power supply type is DC 3.3V from host.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Type DC 3.3V from host

evidence.claim: Radiated emissions testing used test site 03CH03-WS with ambient condition 22-24°C / 66-67% and tester Aska Huang.
The test configuration lists FCC Designation No. TW0009, FCC site registration No. 207696, and IC site registration No. 10807C-1.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC Designation No.: TW0009 FCC site registration No.: 207696 IC site registration No.: 10807C-1

evidence.claim: Measurement uncertainty is ±3.66 dB for radiated emission ≤ 1GHz and ±5.37 dB for radiated emission > 1GHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement Uncertainty Parameters Uncertainty Radiated emission ≤ 1GHz ±3.66 dB Radiated emission > 1GHz ±5.37 dB

evidence.claim: The worst-case orientation for radiated emission measurement was Y-plane.
evidence.evidenceText: The EUT was pretested with 3 orientations placed on the table for the radiated emission measurement – X, Y, and Z-plane. The Y-plane results were found as the worst case and were shown in this report.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is expressed as IC and RSS standards in the source text, while FCC rules and FCC KDB documents are also referenced; jurisdiction should be reviewed if a single authority label is required.
Many result pages for transmitter radiated unwanted emissions contain headings and notes but no extracted numerical tables, suggesting incomplete OCR or omitted tabular data.
Product signal metadata is Unclassified; the source itself identifies the EUT as ST60-2230C-P and also lists SU60-2230C-P in product details.
